  "findings": [
    "Mechanical digging inside tolerance zone without exposing underground utilities by hand or soft digging methods",
    "Contravention of CGA Best Practice 6.0 requiring safe excavation practices within the tolerance zone",
    "Potential for utility damage, service interruption, injury, or fatality"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all mechanical digging immediately within the tolerance zone",
    "Expose all underground utilities via vacuum excavation or hand digging before any further mechanical work",
    "Notify utility owner and site safety officer",
    "Re-evaluate the locate marks and verify depths before resuming excavation",
    "Conduct a safety stand-down and retrain crew on 811/CGA best practices"
  ],
  "rationale": "Mechanical digging within the tolerance zone without first exposing underground utilities is a direct violation of 811/CGA best practices. The work is roughed-in but still active, creating an imminent hazard of utility strikes that can cause severe injury, death, and service outages. Immediate stop-work and corrective exposure are required.",
  "references": [
    "811/CGA Best Practices, Excavation Practice 6.0 – Tolerance Zone",
    "CGA Excavation Manual – Safe Digging Within the Tolerance Zone"
  ]
}
